guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Keep installation commands executable and consistent across languages.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Three primary READMEs and every example install command now include --create-adoption; metadata tests enforce it.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Treat compatibility as evidence-based and avoid claims beyond CI coverage.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Documentation and machine markers distinguish nine verified stacks from four preset-only entries.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Preserve fail-closed lifecycle behavior while increasing test depth.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Direct failure-path tests raised critical module coverage and all governance checks remain fail-closed.

risk.detail: Public onboarding, lifecycle gates, localization, and compatibility evidence were changed with automated regression checks.

observedIssues: The local machine lacks Gradle, PHP/Composer, and .NET, so extended compatibility jobs could not be executed locally.

residualRisks.detail: New Java, Kotlin, Ruby, PHP, and C# jobs require their first GitHub Actions execution to validate runner-specific toolchain integration.
Flutter, Android, and Swift remain explicitly preset-only and require future real-project CI evidence.

reviewReadiness.reason: Local quality and governance checks pass; new extended CI jobs await hosted-runner execution.
expectedReviewFocus: Extended compatibility workflow correctness and runtime cost

boundaryChecks.runtimeEntrypoints: verified: make test and make quality
boundaryChecks.userVisibleOutput: verified: three READMEs and ten examples
boundaryChecks.localization: verified: Japanese instructional prose and automated regression rules

checkpointEvidence.detail: Scope and acceptance confirmed before repository edits.
Implementation reviewed after local quality, release, shell, and diff checks passed.

knownGaps: Hosted CI has not yet executed the five newly added extended stack jobs.
overclaimPrevention: Do not report completion for checks or behavior that were not verified.
